{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_47b896b957b14ed3b2c35270","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Extensible Test Table Structure Guide\n2\t\n3\tThis guide explains how to use and extend ARMOR's table-driven testing framework for different error types.\n4\t\n5\t## Overview\n6\t\n7\tTest tables provide a structured, declarative way to define test cases. Instead of writing individual test functions, you define test cases as data in a table format. This makes tests:\n8\t\n9\t- **DRY**: Define patterns once, reuse across test cases\n10\t- **Maintainable**: Clear separation between test data and logic\n11\t- **Extensible**: Easy to add new test cases by adding table rows\n12\t- **Documented**: Test tables serve as living documentation\n13\t\n14\t## Core Concepts\n15\t\n16\t### TestCase\n17\t\n18\tA `TestCase` represents a single test with inputs, expected outputs, and metadata:\n19\t\n20\t```python\n21\tfrom tests.test_tables import TestCase\n22\t\n23\ttest_case = TestCase(\n24\t    id=\"AUTH-001\",                          # Unique identifier\n25\t    description=\"Missing API key\",          # What this tests\n26\t    input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\"}, # Test inputs\n27\t    expected_status=401,                   # Expected HTTP status\n28\t    expected_error=\"unauthorized\",          # Expected error type\n29\t    expected_message=\"API key required\",    # Expected message (or substring)\n30\t    tags=[\"smoke\", \"auth\"],                 # Categorization tags\n31\t    enabled=True                            # Whether to run this test\n32\t)\n33\t```\n34\t\n35\t### ErrorTestTable\n36\t\n37\tAn `ErrorTestTable` groups related test cases together:\n38\t\n39\t```python\n40\tfrom tests.test_tables import ErrorTestTable\n41\t\n42\ttable = ErrorTestTable(\n43\t    name=\"authentication_errors\",\n44\t    description=\"Test cases for authentication errors\",\n45\t    tags=[\"auth\", \"security\"],\n46\t    test_cases=[test_case1, test_case2, ...]\n47\t)\n48\t```\n49\t\n50\t## Quick Start Examples\n51\t\n52\t### Example 1: Using Pre-configured Tables\n53\t\n54\tARMOR includes pre-configured test tables for common error types:\n55\t\n56\t```python\n57\tfrom tests.test_tables import create_auth_test_table, run_test_table\n58\t\n59\t# Get a pre-configured authentication error table\n60\tauth_table = create_auth_test_table()\n61\t\n62\t# Define an executor function that simulates API requests\n63\tdef mock_executor(input_data):\n64\t    # Simulate API behavior\n65\t    if \"X-API-Key\" not in input_data.get(\"headers\", {}):\n66\t        return {\n67\t            \"status\": 401,\n68\t            \"error\": \"unauthorized\",\n69\t            \"message\": \"API key required\"\n70\t        }\n71\t    return {\"status\": 200, \"error\": None, \"message\": \"OK\"}\n72\t\n73\t# Run all tests in the table\n74\tresults = run_test_table(auth_table, mock_executor)\n75\t\n76\tprint(f\"Passed: {results.passed_count}\")\n77\tprint(f\"Failed: {results.failed_count}\")\n78\tprint(f\"Pass Rate: {results.pass_rate:.1f}%\")\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### Example 2: Creating a Simple Custom Table\n82\t\n83\t```python\n84\tfrom tests.test_tables import ErrorTestTable, TestCase\n85\t\n86\t# Define test cases for a new error type\n87\tcustom_table = ErrorTestTable(\n88\t    name=\"payment_errors\",\n89\t    description=\"Test cases for payment processing errors\",\n90\t    tags=[\"payment\", \"critical\"],\n91\t    test_cases=[\n92\t        TestCase(\n93\t            id=\"PAY-001\",\n94\t            description=\"Invalid credit card number\",\n95\t            input_data={\"card_number\": \"1234\", \"amount\": 100},\n96\t            expected_status=422,\n97\t            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n98\t            expected_message=\"Invalid card number\"\n99\t        ),\n100\t        TestCase(\n101\t            id=\"PAY-002\",\n102\t            description=\"Payment gateway timeout\",\n103\t            input_data={\"card_number\": \"4242424242424242\", \"amount\": 100, \"simulate_timeout\": True},\n104\t            expected_status=504,\n105\t            expected_error=\"gateway_timeout\",\n106\t            expected_message=\"Payment gateway timeout\"\n107\t        ),\n108\t        TestCase(\n109\t            id=\"PAY-003\",\n110\t            description=\"Successful payment\",\n111\t            input_data={\"card_number\": \"4242424242424242\", \"amount\": 100},\n112\t            expected_status=200,\n113\t            expected_error=None\n114\t        )\n115\t    ]\n116\t)\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\t## Creating New Test Tables for Different Error Types\n120\t\n121\t### Pattern 1: Authentication/Authorization Errors\n122\t\n123\tTest API authentication and authorization scenarios:\n124\t\n125\t```python\n126\tfrom tests.test_tables import ErrorTestTable, TestCase\n127\t\n128\tauth_table = ErrorTestTable(\n129\t    name=\"authentication_errors\",\n130\t    description=\"Test cases for authentication and authorization\",\n131\t    tags=[\"auth\", \"security\"],\n132\t    test_cases=[\n133\t        # Missing credentials\n134\t        TestCase(\n135\t            id=\"AUTH-001\",\n136\t            description=\"Request without API key\",\n137\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {}},\n138\t            expected_status=401,\n139\t            expected_error=\"unauthorized\",\n140\t            expected_message=\"API key required\"\n141\t        ),\n142\t\n143\t        # Invalid credentials\n144\t        TestCase(\n145\t            id=\"AUTH-002\",\n146\t            description=\"Invalid API key\",\n147\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"invalid\"}},\n148\t            expected_status=403,\n149\t            expected_error=\"forbidden\",\n150\t            expected_message=\"Invalid API key\"\n151\t        ),\n152\t\n153\t        # Expired credentials\n154\t        TestCase(\n155\t            id=\"AUTH-003\",\n156\t            description=\"Expired API key\",\n157\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"expired-123\"}},\n158\t            expected_status=403,\n159\t            expected_error=\"forbidden\",\n160\t            expected_message=\"API key expired\"\n161\t        ),\n162\t\n163\t        # Insufficient permissions\n164\t        TestCase(\n165\t            id=\"AUTH-004\",\n166\t            description=\"Regular user accessing admin endpoint\",\n167\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/admin\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"user-key\"}},\n168\t            expected_status=403,\n169\t            expected_error=\"forbidden\",\n170\t            expected_message=\"Insufficient permissions\"\n171\t        ),\n172\t\n173\t        # Valid access\n174\t        TestCase(\n175\t            id=\"AUTH-005\",\n176\t            description=\"Valid API key accepted\",\n177\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"valid-key\"}},\n178\t            expected_status=200,\n179\t            expected_error=None,\n180\t            tags=[\"happy-path\"]\n181\t        )\n182\t    ]\n183\t)\n184\t```\n185\t\n186\t### Pattern 2: Validation Errors\n187\t\n188\tTest input validation scenarios:\n189\t\n190\t```python\n191\tvalidation_table = ErrorTestTable(\n192\t    name=\"validation_errors\",\n193\t    description=\"Test cases for input validation\",\n194\t    tags=[\"validation\", \"4xx\"],\n195\t    test_cases=[\n196\t        # Missing required fields\n197\t        TestCase(\n198\t            id=\"VAL-001\",\n199\t            description=\"Missing required email field\",\n200\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"name\": \"John\"}},\n201\t            expected_status=422,\n202\t            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n203\t            expected_message=\"required field\",\n204\t            expected_fields={\"missing_field\": \"email\"}\n205\t        ),\n206\t\n207\t        # Invalid format\n208\t        TestCase(\n209\t            id=\"VAL-002\",\n210\t            description=\"Invalid email format\",\n211\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"email\": \"not-an-email\"}},\n212\t            expected_status=422,\n213\t            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n214\t            expected_message=\"Invalid email format\"\n215\t        ),\n216\t\n217\t        # Out of range\n218\t        TestCase(\n219\t            id=\"VAL-003\",\n220\t            description=\"Age value exceeds maximum\",\n221\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"age\": 150}},\n222\t            expected_status=422,\n223\t            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n224\t            expected_message=\"age must be between\"\n225\t        ),\n226\t\n227\t        # String too long\n228\t        TestCase(\n229\t            id=\"VAL-004\",\n230\t            description=\"Name exceeds max length\",\n231\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"name\": \"x\" * 300}},\n232\t            expected_status=422,\n233\t            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n234\t            expected_message=\"name too long\"\n235\t        ),\n236\t\n237\t        # Valid input\n238\t        TestCase(\n239\t            id=\"VAL-005\",\n240\t            description=\"Valid user creation\",\n241\t            input_data={\n242\t                \"endpoint\": \"/api/users\",\n243\t                \"method\": \"POST\",\n244\t                \"body\": {\"name\": \"Jane\", \"email\": \"jane@example.com\", \"age\": 25}\n245\t            },\n246\t            expected_status=201,\n247\t            expected_error=None,\n248\t            tags=[\"happy-path\"]\n249\t        )\n250\t    ]\n251\t)\n252\t```\n253\t\n254\t### Pattern 3: Resource Not Found Errors\n255\t\n256\tTest 404 scenarios for missing resources:\n257\t\n258\t```python\n259\tnot_found_table = ErrorTestTable(\n260\t    name=\"not_found_errors\",\n261\t    description=\"Test cases for 404 Not Found errors\",\n262\t    tags=[\"404\", \"not_found\"],\n263\t    test_cases=[\n264\t        # Non-existent resource ID\n265\t        TestCase(\n266\t            id=\"NF-001\",\n267\t            description=\"Non-existent user ID\",\n268\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users/999999\"},\n269\t            expected_status=404,\n270\t            expected_error=\"not_found\",\n271\t            expected_message=\"User not found\"\n272\t        ),\n273\t\n274\t        # Invalid path\n275\t        TestCase(\n276\t            id=\"NF-002\",\n277\t            description=\"Non-existent endpoint path\",\n278\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/nonexistent\"},\n279\t            expected_status=404,\n280\t            expected_error=\"not_found\",\n281\t            expected_message=\"Resource not found\"\n282\t        ),\n283\t\n284\t        # Deleted resource\n285\t        TestCase(\n286\t            id=\"NF-003\",\n287\t            description=\"Accessing deleted resource\",\n288\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/posts/1\", \"context\": \"deleted\"},\n289\t            expected_status=404,\n290\t            expected_error=\"not_found\",\n291\t            expected_message=\"Resource has been deleted\"\n292\t        ),\n293\t\n294\t        # Valid resource\n295\t        TestCase(\n296\t            id=\"NF-004\",\n297\t            description=\"Existing resource is accessible\",\n298\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users/1\"},\n299\t            expected_status=200,\n300\t            expected_error=None,\n301\t            tags=[\"happy-path\"]\n302\t        )\n303\t    ]\n304\t)\n305\t```\n306\t\n307\t### Pattern 4: Rate Limiting Errors\n308\t\n309\tTest API rate limiting behavior:\n310\t\n311\t```python\n312\trate_limit_table = ErrorTestTable(\n313\t    name=\"rate_limit_errors\",\n314\t    description=\"Test cases for rate limiting (429) errors\",\n315\t    tags=[\"rate_limit\", \"throttling\"],\n316\t    test_cases=[\n317\t        # Rate limit exceeded\n318\t        TestCase(\n319\t            id=\"RL-001\",\n320\t            description=\"Request rate exceeds limit\",\n321\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/search\", \"request_count\": 101, \"window\": \"1m\"},\n322\t            expected_status=429,\n323\t            expected_error=\"rate_limited\",\n324\t            expected_message=\"Too many requests\",\n325\t            expected_fields={\"retry_after\": 60}\n326\t        ),\n327\t\n328\t        # Retry-After header present\n329\t        TestCase(\n330\t            id=\"RL-002\",\n331\t            description=\"Rate limit includes Retry-After header\",\n332\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/data\", \"request_count\": 1001},\n333\t            expected_status=429,\n334\t            expected_error=\"rate_limited\",\n335\t            expected_message=\"rate limit\",\n336\t            expected_fields={\"headers\": {\"Retry-After\": \"30\"}}\n337\t        ),\n338\t\n339\t        # Within limit\n340\t        TestCase(\n341\t            id=\"RL-003\",\n342\t            description=\"Requests within rate limit accepted\",\n343\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/data\", \"request_count\": 50, \"window\": \"1m\"},\n344\t            expected_status=200,\n345\t            expected_error=None,\n346\t            tags=[\"happy-path\"]\n347\t        )\n348\t    ]\n349\t)\n350\t```\n351\t\n352\t### Pattern 5: Server Errors (5xx)\n353\t\n354\tTest server-side error scenarios:\n355\t\n356\t```python\n357\tserver_error_table = ErrorTestTable(\n358\t    name=\"server_errors\",\n359\t    description=\"Test cases for server error (5xx) responses\",\n360\t    tags=[\"5xx\", \"server_error\", \"critical\"],\n361\t    test_cases=[\n362\t        # Internal server error\n363\t        TestCase(\n364\t            id=\"SE-001\",\n365\t            description=\"Internal server error\",\n366\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/crash\", \"simulate\": \"internal_error\"},\n367\t            expected_status=500,\n368\t            expected_error=\"internal_server_error\",\n369\t            expected_message=\"Internal server error\"\n370\t        ),\n371\t\n372\t        # Database unavailable\n373\t        TestCase(\n374\t            id=\"SE-002\",\n375\t            description=\"Database connection timeout\",\n376\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"simulate\": \"db_timeout\"},\n377\t            expected_status=503,\n378\t            expected_error=\"service_unavailable\",\n379\t            expected_message=\"Database timeout\",\n380\t            tags=[\"database\", \"critical\"]\n381\t        ),\n382\t\n383\t        # External service down\n384\t        TestCase(\n385\t            id=\"SE-003\",\n386\t            description=\"External service unavailable\",\n387\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/external\", \"simulate\": \"upstream_down\"},\n388\t            expected_status=502,\n389\t            expected_error=\"bad_gateway\",\n390\t            expected_message=\"Upstream service unavailable\",\n391\t            tags=[\"external\"]\n392\t        ),\n393\t\n394\t        # Gateway timeout\n395\t        TestCase(\n396\t            id=\"SE-004\",\n397\t            description=\"Gateway timeout\",\n398\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/slow\", \"simulate\": \"timeout\"},\n399\t            expected_status=504,\n400\t            expected_error=\"gateway_timeout\",\n401\t            expected_message=\"Gateway timeout\",\n402\t            tags=[\"timeout\"]\n403\t        )\n404\t    ]\n405\t)\n406\t```\n407\t\n408\t## Advanced Features\n409\t\n410\t### Custom Validators\n411\t\n412\tAdd custom validation logic to a test case:\n413\t\n414\t```python\n415\tdef custom_validator(response):\n416\t    \"\"\"Custom validator that checks response structure.\"\"\"\n417\t    if \"request_id\" not in response.get(\"fields\", {}):\n418\t        return False, \"Response missing request_id field\"\n419\t    return True, None\n420\t\n421\ttest_case = TestCase(\n422\t    id=\"CUSTOM-001\",\n423\t    description=\"Test with custom validation\",\n424\t    input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/custom\"},\n425\t    expected_status=200,\n426\t    custom_validator=custom_validator\n427\t)\n428\t```\n429\t\n430\t### Setup and Teardown Callbacks\n431\t\n432\tDefine setup/teardown logic for test cases:\n433\t\n434\t```python\n435\tdef setup_test(input_data):\n436\t    \"\"\"Setup function run before test execution.\"\"\"\n437\t    print(f\"Setting up test for endpoint: {input_data.get('endpoint')}\")\n438\t\n439\tdef teardown_test(input_data):\n440\t    \"\"\"Teardown function run after test execution.\"\"\"\n441\t    print(f\"Cleaning up after test for endpoint: {input_data.get('endpoint')}\")\n442\t\n443\ttest_case = TestCase(\n444\t    id=\"SETUP-001\",\n445\t    description=\"Test with setup and teardown\",\n446\t    input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/resource\"},\n447\t    expected_status=200,\n448\t    setup_callback=setup_test,\n449\t    teardown_callback=teardown_test\n450\t)\n451\t```\n452\t\n453\t### Filtering by Tags\n454\t\n455\tRun specific subsets of tests based on tags:\n456\t\n457\t```python\n458\tfrom tests.test_tables import create_auth_test_table\n459\t\n460\ttable = create_auth_test_table()\n461\t\n462\t# Get only smoke tests\n463\tsmoke_tests = table.get_test_cases_by_tag(\"smoke\")\n464\t\n465\t# Get only regression tests\n466\tregression_tests = table.get_test_cases_by_tag(\"regression\")\n467\t\n468\t# Get only admin tests\n469\tadmin_tests = table.get_test_cases_by_tag(\"admin\")\n470\t\n471\t# Create filtered table with multiple tags\n472\tfiltered_table = table.filter_by_tags(\"smoke\", \"auth\")\n473\t```\n474\t\n475\t### Disabling Test Cases\n476\t\n477\tTemporarily disable test cases without removing them:\n478\t\n479\t```python\n480\ttest_case = TestCase(\n481\t    id=\"TEMP-001\",\n482\t    description=\"Feature not yet implemented\",\n483\t    input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/new-feature\"},\n484\t    expected_status=200,\n485\t    enabled=False  # This test will be skipped\n486\t)\n487\t```\n488\t\n489\t## Running Tests\n490\t\n491\t### Basic Execution\n492\t\n493\t```python\n494\tfrom tests.test_tables import run_test_table\n495\t\n496\t# Define executor function\n497\tdef executor(input_data):\n498\t    # Your test execution logic here\n499\t    # Returns dict with: status, error, message, fields\n500\t    return {\n501\t        \"status\": 200,\n502\t        \"error\": None,\n503\t        \"message\": \"OK\",\n504\t        \"fields\": {\"request_id\": \"abc123\"}\n505\t    }\n506\t\n507\t# Run tests\n508\tresults = run_test_table(table, executor)\n509\t\n510\t# Check results\n511\tif results.all_passed:\n512\t    print(\"All tests passed!\")\n513\telse:\n514\t    print(f\"Failed: {results.failed_count} tests\")\n515\t    for result in results.results:\n516\t        if result.failed:\n517\t            print(f\"  {result.test_case.id}: {result.error_message}\")\n518\t```\n519\t\n520\t### Stop on First Failure\n521\t\n522\t```python\n523\t# Stop execution on first failure\n524\tresults = run_test_table(table, executor, continue_on_failure=False)\n525\t```\n526\t\n527\t### Table-level Setup/Teardown\n528\t\n529\t```python\n530\tdef table_setup():\n531\t    \"\"\"Setup for entire test table.\"\"\"\n532\t    print(\"Setting up test environment...\")\n533\t\n534\tdef table_teardown():\n535\t    \"\"\"Teardown for entire test table.\"\"\"\n536\t    print(\"Cleaning up test environment...\")\n537\t\n538\ttable = ErrorTestTable(\n539\t    name=\"my_table\",\n540\t    description=\"Test table with setup/teardown\",\n541\t    test_cases=[...],\n542\t    setup_callback=table_setup,\n543\t    teardown_callback=table_teardown\n544\t)\n545\t```\n546\t\n547\t## Best Practices\n548\t\n549\t### 1. Naming Conventions\n550\t\n551\t- **Test IDs**: Use descriptive prefixes (e.g., \"AUTH-001\", \"VAL-001\")\n552\t- **Table names**: Use lowercase with underscores (e.g., \"authentication_errors\")\n553\t- **Tags**: Use lowercase, meaningful categories (e.g., \"smoke\", \"regression\", \"critical\")\n554\t\n555\t### 2. Test Case Organization\n556\t\n557\t- Group related test cases in the same table\n558\t- Use tags to categorize tests by type (smoke, regression, integration)\n559\t- Include both positive (happy path) and negative test cases\n560\t\n561\t### 3. Input Data Structure\n562\t\n563\t- Keep input_data consistent across test cases in a table\n564\t- Use clear, descriptive field names\n565\t- Include all necessary context for the test\n566\t\n567\t### 4. Expected Results\n568\t\n569\t- Always specify expected_status for HTTP tests\n570\t- Use expected_message for substring matching (not exact match)\n571\t- Use expected_fields for additional response validation\n572\t\n573\t### 5. Documentation\n574\t\n575\t- Keep descriptions clear and specific\n576\t- Add tags for easy filtering\n577\t- Include metadata for traceability (e.g., JIRA ticket IDs)\n578\t\n579\t## Real-World Example: Complete API Test Suite\n580\t\n581\t```python\n582\tfrom tests.test_tables import ErrorTestTable, TestCase, run_test_table\n583\t\n584\t# Create comprehensive test suite\n585\tapi_test_suite = ErrorTestTable(\n586\t    name=\"api_comprehensive\",\n587\t    description=\"Complete API test suite\",\n588\t    tags=[\"comprehensive\", \"integration\"],\n589\t    test_cases=[\n590\t        # Authentication tests\n591\t        TestCase(\n592\t            id=\"API-001\",\n593\t            description=\"Valid authentication\",\n594\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/data\", \"token\": \"valid-token\"},\n595\t            expected_status=200,\n596\t            tags=[\"auth\", \"smoke\"]\n597\t        ),\n598\t        TestCase(\n599\t            id=\"API-002\",\n600\t            description=\"Invalid authentication\",\n601\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/data\", \"token\": \"invalid\"},\n602\t            expected_status=401,\n603\t            expected_error=\"unauthorized\",\n604\t            tags=[\"auth\"]\n605\t        ),\n606\t\n607\t        # Validation tests\n608\t        TestCase(\n609\t            id=\"API-003\",\n610\t            description=\"Valid input\",\n611\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"name\": \"John\", \"email\": \"john@example.com\"}},\n612\t            expected_status=201,\n613\t            tags=[\"validation\", \"smoke\"]\n614\t        ),\n615\t        TestCase(\n616\t            id=\"API-004\",\n617\t            description=\"Invalid email format\",\n618\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"name\": \"John\", \"email\": \"invalid\"}},\n619\t            expected_status=422,\n620\t            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n621\t            tags=[\"validation\"]\n622\t        ),\n623\t\n624\t        # Rate limiting tests\n625\t        TestCase(\n626\t            id=\"API-005\",\n627\t            description=\"Within rate limit\",\n628\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/search\", \"request_count\": 10},\n629\t            expected_status=200,\n630\t            tags=[\"rate_limit\", \"smoke\"]\n631\t        ),\n632\t        TestCase(\n633\t            id=\"API-006\",\n634\t            description=\"Exceeds rate limit\",\n635\t            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/search\", \"request_count\": 1000},\n636\t            expected_status=429,\n637\t            expected_error=\"rate_limited\",\n638\t            expected_fields={\"retry_after\": 60},\n639\t            tags=[\"rate_limit\"]\n640\t        )\n641\t    ]\n642\t)\n643\t\n644\t# Run the suite\n645\tdef api_executor(input_data):\n646\t    # Your API testing logic here\n647\t    pass\n648\t\n649\tresults = run_test_table(api_test_suite, api_executor)\n650\t\n651\t# Generate report\n652\tprint(f\"API Test Suite Results:\")\n653\tprint(f\"  Total: {results.total_count}\")\n654\tprint(f\"  Passed: {results.passed_count}\")\n655\tprint(f\"  Failed: {results.failed_count}\")\n656\tprint(f\"  Pass Rate: {results.pass_rate:.1f}%\")\n657\tprint(f\"  Execution Time: {results.execution_time_ms:.0f}ms\")\n658\t```\n659\t\n660\t## Extending for New Error Types\n661\t\n662\tWhen adding support for a new error type:\n663\t\n664\t1. **Identify the error pattern**: What triggers this error? What should the response look like?\n665\t\n666\t2. **Create test cases covering**:\n667\t   - Minimal triggering condition\n668\t   - Edge cases\n669\t   - Valid input (happy path)\n670\t\n671\t3. **Add to a new or existing table**:\n672\t   ```python\n673\t   new_error_table = ErrorTestTable(\n674\t       name=\"new_error_type\",\n675\t       description=\"Test cases for new error type\",\n676\t       test_cases=[...]\n677\t   )\n678\t   ```\n679\t\n680\t4. **Register in collection** (if reusable):\n681\t   ```python\n682\t   from tests.test_tables import ALL_ERROR_TABLES\n683\t   ALL_ERROR_TABLES['new_error_type'] = new_error_table\n684\t   ```\n685\t\n686\t## Testing the Test Tables\n687\t\n688\tCreate test cases that validate your test tables:\n689\t\n690\t```python\n691\timport unittest\n692\tfrom tests.test_tables import create_auth_test_table\n693\t\n694\tclass TestTestTables(unittest.TestCase):\n695\t    def test_auth_table_structure(self):\n696\t        \"\"\"Test that auth table is properly structured.\"\"\"\n697\t        table = create_auth_test_table()\n698\t\n699\t        # Check table metadata\n700\t        self.assertEqual(table.name, \"authentication_errors\")\n701\t        self.assertTrue(len(table.test_cases) > 0)\n702\t\n703\t        # Check test case IDs are unique\n704\t        ids = [tc.id for tc in table.test_cases]\n705\t        self.assertEqual(len(ids), len(set(ids)))\n706\t\n707\t    def test_auth_table_coverage(self):\n708\t        \"\"\"Test that auth table covers all scenarios.\"\"\"\n709\t        table = create_auth_test_table()\n710\t\n711\t        # Check for required test scenarios\n712\t        descriptions = [tc.description.lower() for tc in table.test_cases]\n713\t        self.assertIn(\"missing\", descriptions)\n714\t        self.assertIn(\"invalid\", descriptions)\n715\t```\n716\t\n717\t## Summary\n718\t\n719\tARMOR's extensible test table structure provides:\n720\t\n721\t- **Consistency**: All tests follow the same structure\n722\t- **Reusability**: Test patterns defined once, used everywhere\n723\t- **Maintainability**: Easy to add, modify, or remove test cases\n724\t- **Documentation**: Test tables document expected behavior\n725\t- **Flexibility**: Support for custom validation, setup/teardown, filtering\n726\t\n727\tFor questions or contributions, refer to the main test_tables module documentation.\n728\t\n729\t---\n730\t\n731\t**Bead**: bf-1a04kj\n732\t**Created**: 2026-07-14\n733\t**Updated**: 2026-07-14\n734\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6a685bf6-e45b-4e33-9134-c69d10123a81","uuid":"c4a128a9-a9c9-4335-9648-27c679bd1aa4","timestamp":"2026-07-15T17:54:10.661Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/tests/TEST_TABLE_GUIDE.md","content":"# Extensible Test Table Structure Guide\n\nThis guide explains how to use and extend ARMOR's table-driven testing framework for different error types.\n\n## Overview\n\nTest tables provide a structured, declarative way to define test cases. Instead of writing individual test functions, you define test cases as data in a table format. This makes tests:\n\n- **DRY**: Define patterns once, reuse across test cases\n- **Maintainable**: Clear separation between test data and logic\n- **Extensible**: Easy to add new test cases by adding table rows\n- **Documented**: Test tables serve as living documentation\n\n## Core Concepts\n\n### TestCase\n\nA `TestCase` represents a single test with inputs, expected outputs, and metadata:\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import TestCase\n\ntest_case = TestCase(\n    id=\"AUTH-001\",                          # Unique identifier\n    description=\"Missing API key\",          # What this tests\n    input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\"}, # Test inputs\n    expected_status=401,                   # Expected HTTP status\n    expected_error=\"unauthorized\",          # Expected error type\n    expected_message=\"API key required\",    # Expected message (or substring)\n    tags=[\"smoke\", \"auth\"],                 # Categorization tags\n    enabled=True                            # Whether to run this test\n)\n```\n\n### ErrorTestTable\n\nAn `ErrorTestTable` groups related test cases together:\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import ErrorTestTable\n\ntable = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"authentication_errors\",\n    description=\"Test cases for authentication errors\",\n    tags=[\"auth\", \"security\"],\n    test_cases=[test_case1, test_case2, ...]\n)\n```\n\n## Quick Start Examples\n\n### Example 1: Using Pre-configured Tables\n\nARMOR includes pre-configured test tables for common error types:\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import create_auth_test_table, run_test_table\n\n# Get a pre-configured authentication error table\nauth_table = create_auth_test_table()\n\n# Define an executor function that simulates API requests\ndef mock_executor(input_data):\n    # Simulate API behavior\n    if \"X-API-Key\" not in input_data.get(\"headers\", {}):\n        return {\n            \"status\": 401,\n            \"error\": \"unauthorized\",\n            \"message\": \"API key required\"\n        }\n    return {\"status\": 200, \"error\": None, \"message\": \"OK\"}\n\n# Run all tests in the table\nresults = run_test_table(auth_table, mock_executor)\n\nprint(f\"Passed: {results.passed_count}\")\nprint(f\"Failed: {results.failed_count}\")\nprint(f\"Pass Rate: {results.pass_rate:.1f}%\")\n```\n\n### Example 2: Creating a Simple Custom Table\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import ErrorTestTable, TestCase\n\n# Define test cases for a new error type\ncustom_table = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"payment_errors\",\n    description=\"Test cases for payment processing errors\",\n    tags=[\"payment\", \"critical\"],\n    test_cases=[\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"PAY-001\",\n            description=\"Invalid credit card number\",\n            input_data={\"card_number\": \"1234\", \"amount\": 100},\n            expected_status=422,\n            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n            expected_message=\"Invalid card number\"\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"PAY-002\",\n            description=\"Payment gateway timeout\",\n            input_data={\"card_number\": \"4242424242424242\", \"amount\": 100, \"simulate_timeout\": True},\n            expected_status=504,\n            expected_error=\"gateway_timeout\",\n            expected_message=\"Payment gateway timeout\"\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"PAY-003\",\n            description=\"Successful payment\",\n            input_data={\"card_number\": \"4242424242424242\", \"amount\": 100},\n            expected_status=200,\n            expected_error=None\n        )\n    ]\n)\n```\n\n## Creating New Test Tables for Different Error Types\n\n### Pattern 1: Authentication/Authorization Errors\n\nTest API authentication and authorization scenarios:\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import ErrorTestTable, TestCase\n\nauth_table = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"authentication_errors\",\n    description=\"Test cases for authentication and authorization\",\n    tags=[\"auth\", \"security\"],\n    test_cases=[\n        # Missing credentials\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"AUTH-001\",\n            description=\"Request without API key\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {}},\n            expected_status=401,\n            expected_error=\"unauthorized\",\n            expected_message=\"API key required\"\n        ),\n\n        # Invalid credentials\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"AUTH-002\",\n            description=\"Invalid API key\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"invalid\"}},\n            expected_status=403,\n            expected_error=\"forbidden\",\n            expected_message=\"Invalid API key\"\n        ),\n\n        # Expired credentials\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"AUTH-003\",\n            description=\"Expired API key\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"expired-123\"}},\n            expected_status=403,\n            expected_error=\"forbidden\",\n            expected_message=\"API key expired\"\n        ),\n\n        # Insufficient permissions\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"AUTH-004\",\n            description=\"Regular user accessing admin endpoint\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/admin\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"user-key\"}},\n            expected_status=403,\n            expected_error=\"forbidden\",\n            expected_message=\"Insufficient permissions\"\n        ),\n\n        # Valid access\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"AUTH-005\",\n            description=\"Valid API key accepted\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"valid-key\"}},\n            expected_status=200,\n            expected_error=None,\n            tags=[\"happy-path\"]\n        )\n    ]\n)\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Validation Errors\n\nTest input validation scenarios:\n\n```python\nvalidation_table = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"validation_errors\",\n    description=\"Test cases for input validation\",\n    tags=[\"validation\", \"4xx\"],\n    test_cases=[\n        # Missing required fields\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"VAL-001\",\n            description=\"Missing required email field\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"name\": \"John\"}},\n            expected_status=422,\n            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n            expected_message=\"required field\",\n            expected_fields={\"missing_field\": \"email\"}\n        ),\n\n        # Invalid format\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"VAL-002\",\n            description=\"Invalid email format\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"email\": \"not-an-email\"}},\n            expected_status=422,\n            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n            expected_message=\"Invalid email format\"\n        ),\n\n        # Out of range\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"VAL-003\",\n            description=\"Age value exceeds maximum\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"age\": 150}},\n            expected_status=422,\n            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n            expected_message=\"age must be between\"\n        ),\n\n        # String too long\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"VAL-004\",\n            description=\"Name exceeds max length\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"name\": \"x\" * 300}},\n            expected_status=422,\n            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n            expected_message=\"name too long\"\n        ),\n\n        # Valid input\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"VAL-005\",\n            description=\"Valid user creation\",\n            input_data={\n                \"endpoint\": \"/api/users\",\n                \"method\": \"POST\",\n                \"body\": {\"name\": \"Jane\", \"email\": \"jane@example.com\", \"age\": 25}\n            },\n            expected_status=201,\n            expected_error=None,\n            tags=[\"happy-path\"]\n        )\n    ]\n)\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Resource Not Found Errors\n\nTest 404 scenarios for missing resources:\n\n```python\nnot_found_table = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"not_found_errors\",\n    description=\"Test cases for 404 Not Found errors\",\n    tags=[\"404\", \"not_found\"],\n    test_cases=[\n        # Non-existent resource ID\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"NF-001\",\n            description=\"Non-existent user ID\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users/999999\"},\n            expected_status=404,\n            expected_error=\"not_found\",\n            expected_message=\"User not found\"\n        ),\n\n        # Invalid path\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"NF-002\",\n            description=\"Non-existent endpoint path\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/nonexistent\"},\n            expected_status=404,\n            expected_error=\"not_found\",\n            expected_message=\"Resource not found\"\n        ),\n\n        # Deleted resource\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"NF-003\",\n            description=\"Accessing deleted resource\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/posts/1\", \"context\": \"deleted\"},\n            expected_status=404,\n            expected_error=\"not_found\",\n            expected_message=\"Resource has been deleted\"\n        ),\n\n        # Valid resource\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"NF-004\",\n            description=\"Existing resource is accessible\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users/1\"},\n            expected_status=200,\n            expected_error=None,\n            tags=[\"happy-path\"]\n        )\n    ]\n)\n```\n\n### Pattern 4: Rate Limiting Errors\n\nTest API rate limiting behavior:\n\n```python\nrate_limit_table = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"rate_limit_errors\",\n    description=\"Test cases for rate limiting (429) errors\",\n    tags=[\"rate_limit\", \"throttling\"],\n    test_cases=[\n        # Rate limit exceeded\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"RL-001\",\n            description=\"Request rate exceeds limit\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/search\", \"request_count\": 101, \"window\": \"1m\"},\n            expected_status=429,\n            expected_error=\"rate_limited\",\n            expected_message=\"Too many requests\",\n            expected_fields={\"retry_after\": 60}\n        ),\n\n        # Retry-After header present\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"RL-002\",\n            description=\"Rate limit includes Retry-After header\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/data\", \"request_count\": 1001},\n            expected_status=429,\n            expected_error=\"rate_limited\",\n            expected_message=\"rate limit\",\n            expected_fields={\"headers\": {\"Retry-After\": \"30\"}}\n        ),\n\n        # Within limit\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"RL-003\",\n            description=\"Requests within rate limit accepted\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/data\", \"request_count\": 50, \"window\": \"1m\"},\n            expected_status=200,\n            expected_error=None,\n            tags=[\"happy-path\"]\n        )\n    ]\n)\n```\n\n### Pattern 5: Server Errors (5xx)\n\nTest server-side error scenarios:\n\n```python\nserver_error_table = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"server_errors\",\n    description=\"Test cases for server error (5xx) responses\",\n    tags=[\"5xx\", \"server_error\", \"critical\"],\n    test_cases=[\n        # Internal server error\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"SE-001\",\n            description=\"Internal server error\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/crash\", \"simulate\": \"internal_error\"},\n            expected_status=500,\n            expected_error=\"internal_server_error\",\n            expected_message=\"Internal server error\"\n        ),\n\n        # Database unavailable\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"SE-002\",\n            description=\"Database connection timeout\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"simulate\": \"db_timeout\"},\n            expected_status=503,\n            expected_error=\"service_unavailable\",\n            expected_message=\"Database timeout\",\n            tags=[\"database\", \"critical\"]\n        ),\n\n        # External service down\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"SE-003\",\n            description=\"External service unavailable\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/external\", \"simulate\": \"upstream_down\"},\n            expected_status=502,\n            expected_error=\"bad_gateway\",\n            expected_message=\"Upstream service unavailable\",\n            tags=[\"external\"]\n        ),\n\n        # Gateway timeout\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"SE-004\",\n            description=\"Gateway timeout\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/slow\", \"simulate\": \"timeout\"},\n            expected_status=504,\n            expected_error=\"gateway_timeout\",\n            expected_message=\"Gateway timeout\",\n            tags=[\"timeout\"]\n        )\n    ]\n)\n```\n\n## Advanced Features\n\n### Custom Validators\n\nAdd custom validation logic to a test case:\n\n```python\ndef custom_validator(response):\n    \"\"\"Custom validator that checks response structure.\"\"\"\n    if \"request_id\" not in response.get(\"fields\", {}):\n        return False, \"Response missing request_id field\"\n    return True, None\n\ntest_case = TestCase(\n    id=\"CUSTOM-001\",\n    description=\"Test with custom validation\",\n    input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/custom\"},\n    expected_status=200,\n    custom_validator=custom_validator\n)\n```\n\n### Setup and Teardown Callbacks\n\nDefine setup/teardown logic for test cases:\n\n```python\ndef setup_test(input_data):\n    \"\"\"Setup function run before test execution.\"\"\"\n    print(f\"Setting up test for endpoint: {input_data.get('endpoint')}\")\n\ndef teardown_test(input_data):\n    \"\"\"Teardown function run after test execution.\"\"\"\n    print(f\"Cleaning up after test for endpoint: {input_data.get('endpoint')}\")\n\ntest_case = TestCase(\n    id=\"SETUP-001\",\n    description=\"Test with setup and teardown\",\n    input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/resource\"},\n    expected_status=200,\n    setup_callback=setup_test,\n    teardown_callback=teardown_test\n)\n```\n\n### Filtering by Tags\n\nRun specific subsets of tests based on tags:\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import create_auth_test_table\n\ntable = create_auth_test_table()\n\n# Get only smoke tests\nsmoke_tests = table.get_test_cases_by_tag(\"smoke\")\n\n# Get only regression tests\nregression_tests = table.get_test_cases_by_tag(\"regression\")\n\n# Get only admin tests\nadmin_tests = table.get_test_cases_by_tag(\"admin\")\n\n# Create filtered table with multiple tags\nfiltered_table = table.filter_by_tags(\"smoke\", \"auth\")\n```\n\n### Disabling Test Cases\n\nTemporarily disable test cases without removing them:\n\n```python\ntest_case = TestCase(\n    id=\"TEMP-001\",\n    description=\"Feature not yet implemented\",\n    input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/new-feature\"},\n    expected_status=200,\n    enabled=False  # This test will be skipped\n)\n```\n\n## Running Tests\n\n### Basic Execution\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import run_test_table\n\n# Define executor function\ndef executor(input_data):\n    # Your test execution logic here\n    # Returns dict with: status, error, message, fields\n    return {\n        \"status\": 200,\n        \"error\": None,\n        \"message\": \"OK\",\n        \"fields\": {\"request_id\": \"abc123\"}\n    }\n\n# Run tests\nresults = run_test_table(table, executor)\n\n# Check results\nif results.all_passed:\n    print(\"All tests passed!\")\nelse:\n    print(f\"Failed: {results.failed_count} tests\")\n    for result in results.results:\n        if result.failed:\n            print(f\"  {result.test_case.id}: {result.error_message}\")\n```\n\n### Stop on First Failure\n\n```python\n# Stop execution on first failure\nresults = run_test_table(table, executor, continue_on_failure=False)\n```\n\n### Table-level Setup/Teardown\n\n```python\ndef table_setup():\n    \"\"\"Setup for entire test table.\"\"\"\n    print(\"Setting up test environment...\")\n\ndef table_teardown():\n    \"\"\"Teardown for entire test table.\"\"\"\n    print(\"Cleaning up test environment...\")\n\ntable = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"my_table\",\n    description=\"Test table with setup/teardown\",\n    test_cases=[...],\n    setup_callback=table_setup,\n    teardown_callback=table_teardown\n)\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Naming Conventions\n\n- **Test IDs**: Use descriptive prefixes (e.g., \"AUTH-001\", \"VAL-001\")\n- **Table names**: Use lowercase with underscores (e.g., \"authentication_errors\")\n- **Tags**: Use lowercase, meaningful categories (e.g., \"smoke\", \"regression\", \"critical\")\n\n### 2. Test Case Organization\n\n- Group related test cases in the same table\n- Use tags to categorize tests by type (smoke, regression, integration)\n- Include both positive (happy path) and negative test cases\n\n### 3. Input Data Structure\n\n- Keep input_data consistent across test cases in a table\n- Use clear, descriptive field names\n- Include all necessary context for the test\n\n### 4. Expected Results\n\n- Always specify expected_status for HTTP tests\n- Use expected_message for substring matching (not exact match)\n- Use expected_fields for additional response validation\n\n### 5. Documentation\n\n- Keep descriptions clear and specific\n- Add tags for easy filtering\n- Include metadata for traceability (e.g., JIRA ticket IDs)\n\n## Real-World Example: Complete API Test Suite\n\n```python\nfrom tests.test_tables import ErrorTestTable, TestCase, run_test_table\n\n# Create comprehensive test suite\napi_test_suite = ErrorTestTable(\n    name=\"api_comprehensive\",\n    description=\"Complete API test suite\",\n    tags=[\"comprehensive\", \"integration\"],\n    test_cases=[\n        # Authentication tests\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"API-001\",\n            description=\"Valid authentication\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/data\", \"token\": \"valid-token\"},\n            expected_status=200,\n            tags=[\"auth\", \"smoke\"]\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"API-002\",\n            description=\"Invalid authentication\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/data\", \"token\": \"invalid\"},\n            expected_status=401,\n            expected_error=\"unauthorized\",\n            tags=[\"auth\"]\n        ),\n\n        # Validation tests\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"API-003\",\n            description=\"Valid input\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"name\": \"John\", \"email\": \"john@example.com\"}},\n            expected_status=201,\n            tags=[\"validation\", \"smoke\"]\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"API-004\",\n            description=\"Invalid email format\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"method\": \"POST\", \"body\": {\"name\": \"John\", \"email\": \"invalid\"}},\n            expected_status=422,\n            expected_error=\"validation_error\",\n            tags=[\"validation\"]\n        ),\n\n        # Rate limiting tests\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"API-005\",\n            description=\"Within rate limit\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/search\", \"request_count\": 10},\n            expected_status=200,\n            tags=[\"rate_limit\", \"smoke\"]\n        ),\n        TestCase(\n            id=\"API-006\",\n            description=\"Exceeds rate limit\",\n            input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/search\", \"request_count\": 1000},\n            expected_status=429,\n            expected_error=\"rate_limited\",\n            expected_fields={\"retry_after\": 60},\n            tags=[\"rate_limit\"]\n        )\n    ]\n)\n\n# Run the suite\ndef api_executor(input_data):\n    # Your API testing logic here\n    pass\n\nresults = run_test_table(api_test_suite, api_executor)\n\n# Generate report\nprint(f\"API Test Suite Results:\")\nprint(f\"  Total: {results.total_count}\")\nprint(f\"  Passed: {results.passed_count}\")\nprint(f\"  Failed: {results.failed_count}\")\nprint(f\"  Pass Rate: {results.pass_rate:.1f}%\")\nprint(f\"  Execution Time: {results.execution_time_ms:.0f}ms\")\n```\n\n## Extending for New Error Types\n\nWhen adding support for a new error type:\n\n1. **Identify the error pattern**: What triggers this error? What should the response look like?\n\n2. **Create test cases covering**:\n   - Minimal triggering condition\n   - Edge cases\n   - Valid input (happy path)\n\n3. **Add to a new or existing table**:\n   ```python\n   new_error_table = ErrorTestTable(\n       name=\"new_error_type\",\n       description=\"Test cases for new error type\",\n       test_cases=[...]\n   )\n   ```\n\n4. **Register in collection** (if reusable):\n   ```python\n   from tests.test_tables import ALL_ERROR_TABLES\n   ALL_ERROR_TABLES['new_error_type'] = new_error_table\n   ```\n\n## Testing the Test Tables\n\nCreate test cases that validate your test tables:\n\n```python\nimport unittest\nfrom tests.test_tables import create_auth_test_table\n\nclass TestTestTables(unittest.TestCase):\n    def test_auth_table_structure(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that auth table is properly structured.\"\"\"\n        table = create_auth_test_table()\n\n        # Check table metadata\n        self.assertEqual(table.name, \"authentication_errors\")\n        self.assertTrue(len(table.test_cases) > 0)\n\n        # Check test case IDs are unique\n        ids = [tc.id for tc in table.test_cases]\n        self.assertEqual(len(ids), len(set(ids)))\n\n    def test_auth_table_coverage(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that auth table covers all scenarios.\"\"\"\n        table = create_auth_test_table()\n\n        # Check for required test scenarios\n        descriptions = [tc.description.lower() for tc in table.test_cases]\n        self.assertIn(\"missing\", descriptions)\n        self.assertIn(\"invalid\", descriptions)\n```\n\n## Summary\n\nARMOR's extensible test table structure provides:\n\n- **Consistency**: All tests follow the same structure\n- **Reusability**: Test patterns defined once, used everywhere\n- **Maintainability**: Easy to add, modify, or remove test cases\n- **Documentation**: Test tables document expected behavior\n- **Flexibility**: Support for custom validation, setup/teardown, filtering\n\nFor questions or contributions, refer to the main test_tables module documentation.\n\n---\n\n**Bead**: bf-1a04kj\n**Created**: 2026-07-14\n**Updated**: 2026-07-14\n","numLines":734,"startLine":1,"totalLines":734}}}

       )\n161\t\n162\t\n163\t@dataclass\n164\tclass TestExecutionResult:\n165\t    \"\"\"\n166\t    Result of executing a single test case.\n167\t\n168\t    Contains the outcome, actual vs expected None\n174\t    actual_message: Optional[str] = None\n175\t    actual_fields: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None\n176\t    error_message: Optional[str] = None\n177\t    execution_time_ms: Optional[float] = None\n178\t\n179\t    @property\n180\t    def passed(self) -> bool:\n181\t        \"\"\"Check if the test passed.\"\"\"\n182\t        return self.result == TestResult.PASSED\n183\t\n184\t    @property\n185\t    def failed(self) -> bool:\n186\t        \"\"\"Check if the test failed.\"\"\"\n187\t        return self.result == TestResult.FAILED\n188\t\n189\t    @property\n190\t    def skipped(self) -> bool:\n191\t        \"\"\"Check if the test was skipped.\"\"\"\n192\t        return self.result == TestResult.SKIPPED\n193\t\n194\t    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n195\t        \"\"\"Convert result to dictionary for serialization.\"\"\"\n196\t        return {\n197\t            'test_id': self.test_case.id,\n198\t            'description': self.test_case.description,\n199\t            'result': self.result.value,\n200\t            'expected_status': self.test_case.expected_status,\n201\t            'actual_status': self.actual_status,\n202\t            'expected_error': self.test_case.expected_error,\n203\t            'actual_error': self.actual_error,\n204\t            'expected_message': self.test_case.expected_message,\n205\t            'actual_message': self.actual_message,\n206\t            'error_message': self.error_message,\n207\t            'execution_time_ms': self.execution_time_ms\n208\t        }\n209\t\n210\t\n211\t@dataclass\n212\tclass TestTableResult:\n213\t    \"\"\"\n214\t    Aggregated results from running a test table.\n215\t\n216\t    Contains summary statistics and individual test case results.\n217\t    \"\"\"\n218\t    table_name: str\n219\t    total_count: int\n220\t    passed_count: int\n221\t    failed_count: int\n222\t    skipped_count: int\n223\t    error_count: int\n224\t    results: List[TestExecutionResult] = field(default_factory=list)\n225\t    execution_time_ms: Optional[float] = None\n226\t\n227\t    @property\n228\t    def all_passed(self) -> bool:\n229\t        \"\"\"Check if all tests passed.\"\"\"\n230\t        return self.failed_count == 0 and self.error_count == 0\n231\t\n232\t    @property\n233\t    def pass_rate(self) -> float:\n234\t        \"\"\"Calculate pass rate as a percentage.\"\"\"\n235\t        if self.total_count == 0:\n236\t            return 0.0\n237\t        return (self.passed_count / self.total_count) * 100\n238\t\n239\t    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n240\t        \"\"\"Convert results to dictionary for serialization.\"\"\"\n241\t        return {\n242\t            'table_name': self.table_name,\n243\t            'total': self.total_count,\n244\t            'passed': self.passed_count,\n245\t            'failed': self.failed_count,\n246\t            'skipped': self.skipped_count,\n247\t            'error': self.error_count,\n248\t            'pass_rate': self.pass_rate,\n249\t            'execution_time_ms': self.execution_time_ms,\n250\t            'results': [r.to_dict() for r in self.results]\n251\t        }\n252\t\n253\t\n254\t@dataclass\n255\tclass ErrorTestTable:\n256\t    \"\"\"\n257\t    A collection of test cases for a specific error type or scenario.\n258\t\n259\t    Test tables organize related test cases together and provide metadata\n260\t    about what category of errors they test (authentication, validation, etc.).\n261\t\n262\t    Attributes:\n263\t        name: Unique name for this test table (e.g., \"auth_errors\", \"validation_errors\")\n264\t        description: Human-readable description of what this table tests\n265\t        test_cases: List of test case definitions\n266\t        tags: Optional list of tags for categorization\n267\t        setup_callback: Optional callback to run before the entire table\n268\t        teardown_callback: Optional callback to run after the entire table\n269\t        metadata: Optional dict for additional table metadata\n270\t    \"\"\"\n271\t\n272\t    name: str\n273\t    description: str\n274\t    test_cases: List[TestCase] = field(default_factory=list)\n275\t    tags: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n276\t    setup_callback: Optional[Callable] = None\n277\t    teardown_callback: Optional[Callable] = None\n278\t    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n279\t\n280\t    def __post_init__(self):\n281\t        \"\"\"Validate test table after initialization.\"\"\"\n282\t        if not self.name:\n283\t            raise ValueError(\"Test table must have a name\")\n284\t        if not self.description:\n285\t            raise ValueError(\"Test table must have a description\")\n286\t\n287\t        # Ensure test case IDs are unique within the table\n288\t        ids = [tc.id for tc in self.test_cases]\n289\t        if len(ids) != len(set(ids)):\n290\t            duplicates = [id for id in ids if ids.count(id) > 1]\n291\t            raise ValueError(f\"Duplicate test case IDs found: {set(duplicates)}\")\n292\t\n293\t        # Initialize default test_cases if None\n294\t        if self.test_cases is None:\n295\t            self.test_cases = []\n296\t\n297\t    def add_test_case(self, test_case: TestCase) -> 'ErrorTestTable':\n298\t        \"\"\"Add a test case to this table.\"\"\"\n299\t        # Check for duplicate ID\n300\t        existing_ids = {tc.id for tc in self.test_cases}\n301\t        if test_case.id in existing_ids:\n302\t            raise ValueError(f\"Test case ID '{test_case.id}' already exists in table\")\n303\t\n304\t        self.test_cases.append(test_case)\n305\t        return self\n306\t\n307\t    def get_test_case(self, test_id: str) -> Optional[TestCase]:\n308\t        \"\"\"Get a test case by ID.\"\"\"\n309\t        for tc in self.test_cases:\n310\t            if tc.id == test_id:\n311\t                return tc\n312\t        return None\n313\t\n314\t    def get_test_cases_by_tag(self, tag: str) -> List[TestCase]:\n315\t        \"\"\"Get all test cases with a specific tag.\"\"\"\n316\t        return [tc for tc in self.test_cases if tag in tc.tags]\n317\t\n318\t    def get_enabled_test_cases(self) -> List[TestCase]:\n319\t        \"\"\"Get only enabled test cases.\"\"\"\n320\t        return [tc for tc in self.test_cases if tc.enabled]\n321\t\n322\t    def filter_by_tags(self, *tags: str) -> 'ErrorTestTable':\n323\t        \"\"\"Create a new table with only test cases having all specified tags.\"\"\"\n324\t        filtered_cases = [\n325\t            tc for tc in self.test_cases\n326\t            if all(tag in tc.tags for tag in tags)\n327\t        ]\n328\t        return ErrorTestTable(\n329\t            name=f\"{self.name}_filtered\",\n330\t            description=f\"Filtered view of {self.name}\",\n331\t            test_cases=filtered_cases,\n332\t            tags=self.tags.copy(),\n333\t            metadata=self.metadata.copy()\n334\t        )\n335\t\n336\t    def merge(self, other: 'ErrorTestTable') -> 'ErrorTestTable':\n337\t        \"\"\"Merge another test table into this one.\"\"\"\n338\t        # Check for duplicate IDs\n339\t        self_ids = {tc.id for tc in self.test_cases}\n340\t        other_ids = {tc.id for tc in other.test_cases}\n341\t        overlaps = self_ids & other_ids\n342\t        if overlaps:\n343\t            raise ValueError(f\"Cannot merge tables with overlapping test case IDs: {overlaps}\")\n344\t\n345\t        self.test_cases.extend(other.test_cases)\n346\t        return self\n347\t\n348\t\n349\t# =============================================================================\n350\t# TEST EXECUTION FUNCTIONS\n351\t# =============================================================================\n352\t\n353\tdef run_test_case(\n354\t    test_case: TestCase,\n355\t    executor: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Dict[str, Any]]\n356\t) -> TestExecutionResult:\n357\t    \"\"\"\n358\t    Execute a single test case.\n359\t\n360\t    Args:\n361\t        test_case: The test case to execute\n362\t        executor: Function that takes input_data and returns actual results\n363\t                 Should return dict with optional keys: status, error, message, fields\n364\t\n365\t    Returns:\n366\t        TestExecutionResult: The execution result with pass/fail status\n367\t    \"\"\"\n368\t    import time\n369\t\n370\t    # Check if test is enabled\n371\t    if not test_case.enabled:\n372\t        return TestExecutionResult(\n373\t            test_case=test_case,\n374\t            result=TestResult.SKIPPED\n375\t        )\n376\t\n377\t    start_time = time.time()\n378\t\n379\t    try:\n380\t        # Run setup callback if provided\n381\t        if test_case.setup_callback:\n382\t            test_case.setup_callback(test_case.input_data)\n383\t\n384\t        # Execute the test\n385\t        actual = executor(test_case.input_data)\n386\t\n387\t        # Extract actual values\n388\t        actual_status = actual.get('status')\n389\t        actual_error = actual.get('error')\n390\t        actual_message = actual.get('message')\n391\t        actual_fields = actual.get('fields')\n392\t\n393\t        # Validate expected status\n394\t        if test_case.expected_status is not None:\n395\t            if actual_status != test_case.expected_status:\n396\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n397\t                    test_case=test_case,\n398\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n399\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n400\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n401\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n402\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n403\t                    error_message=f\"Expected status {test_case.expected_status}, got {actual_status}\"\n404\t                )\n405\t\n406\t        # Validate expected error\n407\t        if test_case.expected_error is not None:\n408\t            if actual_error != test_case.expected_error:\n409\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n410\t                    test_case=test_case,\n411\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n412\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n413\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n414\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n415\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n416\t                    error_message=f\"Expected error '{test_case.expected_error}', got '{actual_error}'\"\n417\t                )\n418\t\n419\t        # Validate expected message\n420\t        if test_case.expected_message is not None:\n421\t            if actual_message is None or test_case.expected_message not in actual_message:\n422\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n423\t                    test_case=test_case,\n424\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n425\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n426\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n427\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n428\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n429\t                    error_message=f\"Expected message containing '{test_case.expected_message}', got '{actual_message}'\"\n430\t                )\n431\t\n432\t        # Validate expected fields\n433\t        if test_case.expected_fields is not None:\n434\t            if actual_fields is None:\n435\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n436\t                    test_case=test_case,\n437\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n438\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n439\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n440\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n441\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n442\t                    error_message=f\"Expected fields {test_case.expected_fields}, but no fields returned\"\n443\t                )\n444\t            for key, expected_value in test_case.expected_fields.items():\n445\t                if key not in actual_fields:\n446\t                    return TestExecutionResult(\n447\t                        test_case=test_case,\n448\t                        result=TestResult.FAILED,\n449\t                        actual_status=actual_status,\n450\t                        actual_error=actual_error,\n451\t                        actual_message=actual_message,\n452\t                        actual_fields=actual_fields,\n453\t                        error_message=f\"Missing expected field: {key}\"\n454\t                    )\n455\t                if actual_fields[key] != expected_value:\n456\t                    return TestExecutionResult(\n457\t                        test_case=test_case,\n458\t                        result=TestResult.FAILED,\n459\t                        actual_status=actual_status,\n460\t                        actual_error=actual_error,\n461\t                        actual_message=actual_message,\n462\t                        actual_fields=actual_fields,\n463\t                        error_message=f\"Field '{key}' expected {expected_value}, got {actual_fields[key]}\"\n464\t                    )\n465\t\n466\t        # Run custom validator if provided\n467\t        if test_case.custom_validator:\n468\t            passed, error_msg = test_case.custom_validator(actual)\n469\t            if not passed:\n470\t                return TestExecutionResult(\n471\t                    test_case=test_case,\n472\t                    result=TestResult.FAILED,\n473\t                    actual_status=actual_status,\n474\t                    actual_error=actual_error,\n475\t                    actual_message=actual_message,\n476\t                    actual_fields=actual_fields,\n477\t                    error_message=error_msg or \"Custom validation failed\"\n478\t                )\n479\t\n480\t        # Test passed\n481\t        execution_time = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n482\t        return TestExecutionResult(\n483\t            test_case=test_case,\n484\t            result=TestResult.PASSED,\n485\t            actual_status=actual_status,\n486\t            actual_error=actual_error,\n487\t            actual_message=actual_message,\n488\t            actual_fields=actual_fields,\n489\t            execution_time_ms=execution_time\n490\t        )\n491\t\n492\t    except Exception as e:\n493\t        # Test error\n494\t        execution_time = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n495\t        return TestExecutionResult(\n496\t            test_case=test_case,\n497\t            result=TestResult.ERROR,\n498\t            error_message=f\"Test execution error: {str(e)}\",\n499\t            execution_time_ms=execution_time\n500\t        )\n501\t\n502\t    finally:\n503\t        # Run teardown callback if provided\n504\t        if test_case.teardown_callback:\n505\t            try:\n506\t                test_case.teardown_callback(test_case.input_data)\n507\t            except Exception as e:\n508\t                # Don't fail the test on teardown error, just log it\n509\t                pass\n510\t\n511\t\n512\tdef run_test_table(\n513\t    table: ErrorTestTable,\n514\t    executor: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], Dict[str, Any]],\n515\t    continue_on_failure: bool = True\n516\t) -> TestTableResult:\n517\t    \"\"\"\n518\t    Run all test cases in a test table.\n519\t\n520\t    Args:\n521\t        table: The test table to run\n522\t        executor: Function that executes test cases and returns results\n523\t        continue_on_failure: If True, continues running tests after failures\n524\t\n525\t    Returns:\n526\t        TestTableResult: Aggregated results from all test cases\n527\t    \"\"\"\n528\t    import time\n529\t\n530\t    start_time = time.time()\n531\t    results = []\n532\t\n533\t    # Run table setup callback\n534\t    if table.setup_callback:\n535\t        try:\n536\t            table.setup_callback()\n537\t        except Exception as e:\n538\t            # If table setup fails, return error result\n539\t            return TestTableResult(\n540\t                table_name=table.name,\n541\t                total_count=len(table.test_cases),\n542\t                passed_count=0,\n543\t                failed_count=0,\n544\t                skipped_count=len(table.test_cases),\n545\t                error_count=len(table.test_cases),\n546\t                execution_time_ms=(time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n547\t            )\n548\t\n549\t    # Run each test case\n550\t    for test_case in table.test_cases:\n551\t        result = run_test_case(test_case, executor)\n552\t        results.append(result)\n553\t\n554\t        # Stop on first failure if configured\n555\t        if not continue_on_failure and result.result in (TestResult.FAILED, TestResult.ERROR):\n556\t            # Mark remaining tests as skipped\n557\t            for remaining_case in table.test_cases[len(results):]:\n558\t                results.append(TestExecutionResult(\n559\t                    test_case=remaining_case,\n560\t                    result=TestResult.SKIPPED\n561\t                ))\n562\t            break\n563\t\n564\t    # Run table teardown callback\n565\t    if table.teardown_callback:\n566\t        try:\n567\t            table.teardown_callback()\n568\t        except Exception as e:\n569\t            pass  # Don't fail the table run on teardown error\n570\t\n571\t    # Calculate summary statistics\n572\t    passed_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.PASSED)\n573\t    failed_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.FAILED)\n574\t    skipped_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.SKIPPED)\n575\t    error_count = sum(1 for r in results if r.result == TestResult.ERROR)\n576\t\n577\t    return TestTableResult(\n578\t        table_name=table.name,\n579\t        total_count=len(results),\n580\t        passed_count=passed_count,\n581\t        failed_count=failed_count,\n582\t        skipped_count=skipped_count,\n583\t        error_count=error_count,\n584\t        results=results,\n585\t        execution_time_ms=(time.time() - start_time) * 1000\n586\t    )\n587\t\n588\t\n589\t# =============================================================================\n590\t# CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR CREATING TEST TABLES\n591\t# =============================================================================\n592\t\n593\tdef create_simple_test_table(\n594\t    name: str,\n595\t    description: str,\n596\t    test_data: List[Tuple[str, str, Dict[str, Any], int, str, str]]\n597\t) -> ErrorTestTable:\n598\t    \"\"\"\n599\t    Create a test table from a simple list format.\n600\t\n601\t    Args:\n602\t        name: Table name\n603\t        description: Table description\n604\t        test_data: List of tuples in format:\n605\t                   (id, description, input_data, expected_status, expected_error, expected_message)\n606\t\n607\t    Returns:\n608\t        ErrorTestTable: Configured test table\n609\t    \"\"\"\n610\t    test_cases = []\n611\t    for i, (test_id, desc, inputs, status, error, message) in enumerate(test_data, 1):\n612\t        test_cases.append(TestCase(\n613\t            id=test_id,\n614\t            description=desc,\n615\t            input_data=inputs,\n616\t            expected_status=status,\n617\t            expected_error=error,\n618\t            expected_message=message\n619\t        ))\n620\t\n621\t    return ErrorTestTable(\n622\t        name=name,\n623\t        description=description,\n624\t        test_cases=test_cases\n625\t    )\n626\t\n627\t\n628\tdef create_auth_test_table() -> ErrorTestTable:\n629\t    \"\"\"\n630\t    Create a comprehensive test      tags=[\"missing-credentials\", \"basic-auth\"]\n674\t            ),\n675\t            # Invalid credentials test cases\n676\t            TestCase(\n677\t                id=\"AUTH-004\",\n678\t expected_status=403,\n681\t                expected_error=\"forbidden\",\n682\t                expected_message=\"Invalid API key\",\n683\t                tags=[\"invalid-credentials\", input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"Authorization\": \"Bearer invalid-token-123\"}},\n689\t                expected_status=401,\n690\t                expected_error=\"unauthorized\",\n691\t                expected_message=\"Invalid authentication token\",\n692\t                tags=[\"invalid-credentials\", \"bearer-token\"]\n693\t            ),\n694\t            TestCase(\n695\t                id=\"AUTH-006\",\n696\t                description=\"Revoked API key\",\n697\t                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\",    id=\"AUTH-007\",\n706\t                description=\"Expired Bearer token\",\n707\t                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"Authorization\": \"Bearer expected_message=\"token expired\",\n711\t                tags=[\"expired-credentials\", \"bearer-token\"]\n712\t            ),\n713\t            TestCase(\n714\t                id=\"AUTH-008\",\n715\t                description=\"Expired API key\",\n716\t                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/users\", \"headers\": {\"X-API-Key\": \"expired-key-abc\"}, id=\"AUTH-009\",\n725\t                description=\"Wrong password for basic authentication\",\n726\t                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/auth/login\", \"username\": \"user@example.com\",              id=\"AUTH-010\",\n735\t                description=\"Wrong password with account lockout warning\",\n736\t                input_data={\"endpoint\": \"/api/auth/login\", \"username\": python3\n\"\"\"\nExtensible Test Table Structure for ARMOR Tests\n\nThis module provides a reusable, table-driven testing framework for ARMOR test cases.\nTest tables allow you to define test cases in a structured, declarative way that can\nbe easily extended for different error types and scenarios.\n\nStructure:\n- TestCase: Individual test case with inputs, expected outputs, and metadata\n- ErrorTestTable: Collection of test cases for a specific error type or scenario\n- Test execution functions: Run tests from tables with proper validation\n\nBenefits:\n- DRY: Define test patterns once, reuse across multiple test cases\n- Extensible: Easy to add new test cases by adding rows to the table\n- Maintainable: Clear separation between test data and test logic\n- Documented: Test tables serve as living documentation of expected behavior\n\nUsage Example:\n    >>> # Define a test table for authentication errors\n    >>> auth_table = ErrorTestTable(\n    ...     name=\"Authentication Errors\",\n    ...     description=\"Test cases for authentication-related error responses\",\n    ...     test_cases=[\n    ... ]\n    ... )\n    >>>\n    >>> # Run all tests in the table\n    >>> results = run_test_table(auth_table)\n    >>> print(f\"Passed: {results.passed_count}, Failed: {results.failed_count}\")\n\nBead: TestCase:\n    \"\"\"\n    A single test case definition.\n\n    Represents one row in a test table with all the information needed\n    to execute the test and validate the result.\n\n    Attributes:\n        id: Unique identifier for this test case (e.g., \"AUTH-001\", \"VAL-001\")\n        description: Human-readable description of what this test validates\n        input_data: Input parameters for the test (endpoint, headers, body, etc.)\n        expected_status: Expected HTTP status code (None if not testing HTTP)\n        expected_error: Expected error type/identifier (e.g., \"not_found\", \"unauthorized\")\n        expected_message: Expected error message (or substring of message)\n        expected_fields: Optional dict of additional fields to validate in response\n        tags: Optional list of tags for categorization (e.g., [\"smoke\", \"auth\", \"critical\"])\n        enabled: Whether this test case is enabled (disabled tests are skipped)\n        setup_callback: Optional callback function to run before test execution\n        teardown_callback: Optional callback function to run after test execution\n        custom_validator: Optional custom validation function for this test case\n        metadata: Optional dict for additional test metadata\n    \"\"\"\n\n    id: str\n    description: str\n    input_data: Dict[str, Any]\n    expected_status: field(default_factory=list)\n    enabled: bool = True\n    setup_callback: Optional[Callable] = None\n    teardown_callback: Optional[Callable] = None\n    custom_validator: Optional[Callable[[Any], Tuple[bool, str]]] = None\n    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def __post_init__(self):\n        \"\"\"Validate test case after initialization.\"\"\"\n        if not self.id:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test case must have an id\")\n        if not self.description:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test case must have a description\")\n        if self.input_data is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"Test case must have input_data\")\n\n    @property\n    def is_http_test(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if this is an HTTP status test.\"\"\"\n        return self.expected_status is not None\n\n    @property\n    def is_error_test(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if this tests error responses.\"\"\"\n        return self.expected_error is not None\n\n    def with_tags(self, *tags: str) -> 'TestCase':\n        \"\"\"Return a new test case with additional tags.\"\"\"\n        new_tags = list(set(self.tags + list(tags)))\n        return TestCase(\n            id=self.id,\n            description=self.description,\n            input_data=self.input_data,\n            expected_status=self.expected_status,\n            expected_error=self.expected_error,\n            expected_message=self.expected_message,\n            expected_fields=self.expected_fields,\n            tags=new_tags,\n            enabled=self.enabled,\n            setup_callback=self.setup_callback,\n            teardown_callback=self.teardown_callback,\n            custom_validator=self.custom_validator,\n            metadata=self.metadata.copy()\n        )\n\n    def with_metadata(self, **metadata) -> 'TestCase':\n        \"\"\"Return a new test case with additional metadata.\"\"\"\n        new_metadata =
